title: Cyclosorus × rolandii
taxonomy: Cyclosorus × rolandii is a hybrid species within the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Cyclosorus, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the 'iron ferns.' The classification of Cyclosorus × rolandii is still under scientific research and review. Further study is required to confirm its classification within the plant kingdom. However, it is currently considered a member of the division Pteridophyta.
uses: Cyclosorus × rolandii is known for its potential medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. Its extracts have been studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions. intro: Cyclosorus × rolandii, also known as Roland's maiden fern or Roland's spleenwort, is a hybrid species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, which include treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus × rolandii include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal and antioxidant activities. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and infusates, which are often employed in folk medicine to alleviate symptoms associated with infections and other health conditions.
